comments: Tony Wilson, "I don't know, no answer, goodnight everybody!".

sessions.details: Sessions recorded in 1965 (w. Eric Clapton) and 1968 (w. Jimmy Page).

track_listing.details: LP-Wild Cat, played from an acetate
LP-Wild Cat, played from an acetate

transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
This is Thomas the Clown here, TV on the radio and welcome once again to the Friday Rock Show.
And this evening out of the archives from the 60s we feature one of the first and the last session
ever recorded for the BBC by the Yardbirds. They feature Eric Clapton and Jimmy Page.
Also a repeat of our ace session by Taurus. And we got classic cuts from Black Oak Arkansas,
ZZ Top, ACDC and Boston, among others,
and new releases by Samson, Judas Priest, Michael Schenker,
Tigers of Pantang and Diamondhead.
And a track from Drama, the new LP by Yes, the brand new line-up.
But this evening, we start with a classic by Mountain.
Well, I bet you money if you haven't got that album, you don't know who that is.
That was Samson, and it's a track from their new album, which is out on Gem Records.
The album's called Head On, and the track you heard was Walking Out On You.
And before that, Mountain, a real classic from Yonks Ago, Nantucket's sleigh ride to Owen Coffin.
And that was played specially for Pete and Bartley from Swansea.
And also for Robert Ingham, a.k.a. Big Bad Bob.
He lives in Grimsby and he wants me to say hello to the infamous Stella of Howell.
And also we played that track by Mountain for Andrew Harding, who lives in Hainault in Essex.
10.14 on the Friday Rock Show, here's the first of four from our repeat session by Taurus.

The record got a terrible slating on Roundtable, I don't really know why, because I think it's one of the best records they've ever made, and ultimately it's going to become their anthem, wherever they appear live, I would imagine, everybody's going to know the words, they'll stand up and they'll sway and they'll sing it, united.
Judas Priest and their new single, which of course is also on their album British Steel, and before that the first of four from our repeat session by the band called Taurus, the track was Fast Car.
10.21 on the Friday Rock Show.
A nice bit of criticism about a record we played last week.
It comes from Stephen Ainslie of Chelmsford.
He just wrote P.S. on his letter.
Holly Course Heavy Metal Mania kept me amused for the rest of the show last night.
Will you have the Baron Knights on next week?
If they do a heavy metal send-up, maybe we will one day.
Also I'd like to mention a lady who's too shy to have her name mentioned.
Her name is N. Onimus.
And I'd like to say a very good evening to another lady who's very shy.
But I do know her name. Her name is Helen.
And here's a little-known track by Black Oak, Arkansas.
On their one and only performance in this country last year,
they really were excellent.
If you missed them at the Hammersmith Odeon,
then you missed something that was really, really special.
That was ZZ Top from their album called Fandango,
and the track was Tush.
That was specially for two strange people
who have very bad handwriting,
namely Tommy Vance and Tony Wilson,
because it's a very favourite track of those two strange people.
And also it's an intriguing song
because it's turning up in the sets of many, many people at the moment.
Many new bands.
For instance, it's on the B-side of the new Tigers of Pantang single.
That's something I must ask them about when I meet them next week for Rock on Saturday.
And also, in the Girl's School set, they do it twice.
At least Tony Wilson said they did it twice when he was watching them on stage at Folkestone.
I think he's in love with a lead guitarist, but that's another story.
And Girl's School are finishing their current tour at the Civic Hall in St Albans tomorrow night.
And another band is supporting them, who've been on this show, a band by the name of More.
Prior to that, you heard Black Oak, Arkansas, and a track from Balls of Fire.
The track was rock and roll, and that was for Gary and YAT
and all the Hieronymus Bosch fans worldwide.
1029, back to 1965 and the Yardbirds.
For your love, for your love, for your love
Mind you give, give the sun and the stars for I live
For your love, choose freely with delight
I'll give you diamonds bright, baby days I will excite
Make a dream of me at night
For your love
For your love
That's what I've got to say
If I say thanks for spending
My money
That's one thing I can do without
Talking
That's what I've got to say
Is to think I knew
Things like I had a chatter
Don't call me baby I'll call you
That's what I've got to say
If I say thanks for spending
I ain't gonna say nothing yet
And here's the first number on the show by that great group, the Yardbirds.
The Yardbirds had an archive session that featured the work of Mr. Eric Clapton on lead guitar.
And these are the tracks that we played for you.
First was For Your Love, then I'm Not Talking, then I Wish You Would, and then Heart Full of Soul.
And we got another session by the Yardbirds coming up later on in the program that features Mr. Jimmy Page.
And now here's a track by ACDC.
was recorded on an album called TNT
that came out in Australia way back in 1975,
and I don't think this track has been included
on any other album that's been released.
In other words, I don't think it's been put out in this country.
It's called Rocker.
ACDC, from the album that jumped right in at number one this week
in the top 30 album chart, the album called Back in Black.
The track was Harold's Bells.
And before that, something from an Australian import,
the album called TNT that came out in 1975 featuring ACDC.
That track was called Rocker.
And incidentally, you can get both of those Australian imports,
that's the Australian version of High Voltage and TNT,
through Pacific Records in London.
Play that whole ACDC set for everybody
who's going to join the sixth form at Ashington High
up there in Northumberland.
Best wishes from Ashby Davidson.
And also for a gentleman by the name of Alastair Gibson
who lives in Eatonwick near Windsor in Berkshire.
Nice part of the world, that is.
And for Mick Parker and Steve Brindle and Dave Knott
and any of the Harwood heavies who may be listening,
up there in bolton lots of best wishes from phil finch and also for donald stark who lives in
glasgow and finally for everybody going into the fifth year at people's high i didn't know
andy was into the school business that's pin and mike and angus and hardy best wishes from
andrew connor here's taurus and you know how i'm feeling the album's coming out because there's
no release date on the white label that we're playing it playing it from rather the album is
by Michael Schenker
and that track
you heard there
was called
Cry for the Nation
and it's coming out
shortly on Chrysalis
and before that
you heard Taurus
and the track called
You Know How I'm Feeling
3 minutes to 11
it's time for the
Friday Night Connection
right
the tracks we played
you last week
were these
Parallels
by Yes
from Going for the One
recorded in Montreux
in Switzerland
Smoke on the Water
by Deep Purple
with reference to
We All Came Out
to Montreux
and also to Swiss Time
and then Heat Strokes
by Crocus
from Metal Rendezvous,
Crocus being a Swiss-based band.
And a Maltese lead singer.
Loads of people wrote in,
and thanks to all of you who did.
Every letter was read,
I promise you that, this afternoon.
But as usual, there can only be one winner
because we're running short of money around here.
And the winner this week is Dave Bailey
of number two, Ringgold's Way,
Street in Somerset.
Dave, congratulations.
You get yourself a £10 record token.
Our compliments.
And now, with our compliments,
here's the Friday Night Connection
put together by Dave Bailey.
Three bits of music, and there certainly is a theme somewhere in them.
Street and Somerset, a nice selection and a clever idea.
If you think you know what the Friday Night Connection is,
if it has crept into your imagination,
then put down the answer on a postcard or in a letter
or any form of communication you like and send it to
The Friday Night Connection,
The Friday Rock Show, BBC Radio 1, London, W1A 4WW.
You win the Friday Night Connection, you get a £10 record token.
Ten minutes after eleven is our time here on the Friday Rock Show.
And I announced on this programme some many weeks ago now
that John Anderson and Rick Wakeman had left Yes
and the Buggles were going to join up with the remainder of Yes.
There was a heck of a reaction.
Now, I know pretty well that the reaction was,
you're joking, it's not going to work.
Well, now you can make your mind up
because here's a track from their forthcoming album.
It comes out on the 22nd of this month
Have a listen to this
It's magical and it's called Into The Lens
That's from the forthcoming album by the new line-up of Yes
Downs, Horn, Howe, Squire and White
The album's going to be called Drama
And the track you just heard was Into The Lens
Now I've got one up on you
Yesterday afternoon I was invited to the office of one Foxy Fowler
As he's known in the business
Who's the head of promotion for Atlantic Records, their record company
and he played me the whole of the album
and I sat there thinking as a lot of people
are going to be really sort of surprised
because it works.
The Buggles and Yes conjoining really does work.
It's a masterpiece of an album
but you're the people who'll decide whether you like it or not.
In my estimation it'll go straight to number one.
As soon as we get other tracks in the album
I'll play them for you.
And may I say as well lots of luck to John Anderson
in everything that he does from now on.
We've been humbled by our listeners.
we made a mistake rockers by acdc or rather rocker by acdc is on release in this country
it's on the album dirty deeds done cheap just about the only album we didn't check
can't win them all but we try from the album boston whatever happened to them and that was
for philip hill the mole and stone the loach and amos the 14 year old heavy metal freaks from
batley grammar school and also pigged in the big one and gigs in the future foray at batley grammar
school as well as scoffy and lugsy and shocker the punks and also it was for everybody at ride
high school over there on the isle of wight thanks for your hospitality the other night
best wishes from keith button and sarah stevens i've got to say that you're the girl in the life
of roger and he loves you also played that boston track for philip morgan and philip lives in andover
in hans well i've been asked just to mention gordon is no it's uh michael and david gordon
Lots of best wishes from Anne.
Prior to that, you heard something by Taurus from our repeat session by them.
The track was Shy Girl.
The Tigers of Pantang are bringing out their album very shortly,
and here's a couple of tracks from it.
It features the Tigers of Pantang, two tracks from the album,
and it's coming out shortly.
The first one was Wildcats, and the second one was Slave to Freedom.
Sorry about the jump.
We were playing those off acetate,
and they're the worst things in the world to queue up on record turntables in studios.
Nonetheless, that was recorded, incidentally, when they were a four-piece.
Now they're a five-piece, and they incorporate another guitarist by the name of John Sykes,
and he's going to be making his debut with the band at Reading.
Right, back into the archives, way back to 1968.
This session features Mr. Jimmy Page, and it's by the Yardbirds.
Every night she goes out with men to travel
In the carnets of ecstasy
Good night, sweet Josephine
Listen, no one else quite like you

I know that she would, love to

And whether or not you like what she's got
She's guaranteed not to blame you
Yardbirds one more time with that super special Yardbirds sound
And a song of their own called My Baby
That was the last session they ever recorded for the BBC
Way back in 1968, the Yardbirds
The three tracks were Think About It, Good Night Sweet Josephine and My Baby
And that was specially for all Yardbirds freaks
who wrote in and demanded we play something by them out of the archives.
Here's Diamond Head.
From an album that's coming out shortly on Media Records,
it's by Diamond Head, and the track we played you there was called Am I Evil?
And I have a feeling we're going to be playing more from that album in the weeks that follow.
We're going to finish tonight with a track by Taurus, which is called Paper Chaser,
but just before I start to play it, let me tell you what Taurus are doing.
Gig-wise, on the 11th of August, the Old Kent Road at Redcar,
18th of August, London Music Machine,
19th and 20th of August at the London Marquee,
21st of August at the Bristol Granary Club
23rd, Marshall Rooms in Stroud
or Stroud
24th of August, Effington Park Manor
Stratford-upon-Avon
and also they are at the moment in the process of getting a major record deal
this track has been requested by a lot of people again
it's Taurus and Paper Chaser
that's another of the tracks you're going to find on Metal Explosion
an LP coming out soon
and that's it for this edition of the Friday Rock Show
which I sincerely hope you enjoyed
I'd like to know what you think about the new Yes
let me know in the letters
Our engineer tonight was Malcolm.
Malcolm, we thank you for your help.
Our producer is Tony Wilson, who's been working since 7.30,
because he also produces Annie Nightingale at the moment.
He's walking around with stars in his eyes.
I'm going to ask him why.
Why, Wilson, why?
No, no answer.
Good night, everybody.
Gotcha.
Don't forget, rock on Saturday, tomorrow, 4 to 7.30.
I'm Tommy Vance in London.
Wherever you are, I wish you well.
Tonight, from all of us on Radio 1.
And God bless, and back into the clown's uniform.
